name: Siam (1916–1917)
description: A red field with two horizontal white stripes — a five-band design of red, white, red, white, red — the civil flag preceding the trairanga tricolour.
history: In 1916 Rama VI replaced the white-elephant flag with a simpler banded design of red and white. It served as the civil flag until September 1917, when the central red band was changed to blue to create the present red-white-blue trairanga (thong trairong).
